headlines.title: Authorities investigate stabbing after graduation ceremony in Daly City
headlines.description: Authorities are investigating a stabbing that ocurred following a graduation ceremony at Westmoor High School on Friday, according to the school. Officials said the stabbing occurred on campus following the ceremony, which was scheduled to begin at 2:30 p.m. They said police and emergency personnel responded and remain on site as they continue their investigation. School authorities said they are cooperating with law enforcement.

headlines.title: The Culture Keepers: New effort to support artists who shape Bay Area's identity
headlines.description: A new Bay Area project is putting both money and attention behind the artists and cultural leaders shaping the region’s identity. The Culture Keepers is a new initiative investing nearly $1 million into local artists, nonprofits and cultural institutions working to preserve community and culture. The multi-year initiative is backed by a single donor providing long-term support for artists and cultural spaces across the Bay Area. Organizers say the effort comes at a critical time as rising costs and cuts to arts funding continue to push creatives out of the communities they help shape. “We think it is really critical in this moment in time that we continue to create those creative workforce opportunities for individual artists,” The Culture Keepers Chief Curator Vanessa Camarena-Arredondo said. “Arts and culture does not thrive if we don’t have work for them and artists are not cared for on a day to day.” The project also helps fund storytellers and filmmakers. Organizers say the goal is to support those driving culture while acknowledging art as essential community infrastructure. “We felt the urgency of the moment,” Camarena-Arredondo said. “Not only that, but realized a lot of our artists and culture groups, they don’t have the funds to spread their story. We wanted to help them spread their story about the impact of culture in our region and put an investment behind them where they felt supported.” NBC Bay Area’s Velena Jones has more in the video report above.

headlines.title: Man charged with murder in San Francisco shooting of man in wheelchair
headlines.description: A 28-year-old man has been accused in the killing of a man in a wheelchair who was shot in the back of the head in San Francisco’s Mission District. Darryon Harry Chun was charged with using an untraceable pistol, known as a ghost gun, in the slaying about 5 a.m. on Aug. 4, 2024, near the intersection of 20th Street and Shotwell Street, prosecutors said. Chun was also accused of resisting arrest and possession of an assault weapon, the San Franciso District Attorney’s Office said Friday. In surveillance video, “two men are seen approaching Mr. Chun, who then allegedly drew a firearm and pointed it at the pair,” prosecutors said. “The witness is seen running away, and the victim attempted to roll away but was unsuccessful.” “The video allegedly shows Mr. Chun then rapidly approaching the victim. Shortly afterwards, an out-of-frame gunshot is heard,” according to the District Attorney’s Office. Police subsequently arrested Chun and searched a residence where they found an illegal AR-15 assault rifle and a privately manufactured pistol modeled after a Sig Sauer P320, prosecutors said. Anyone with information regarding the case is asked to call the San Francisco Police Department (415) 575-4444 or send a text to to TIP411 and begin the message with SFPD.

headlines.title: Police arrest 3 suspects in downtown San Jose shooting
headlines.description: Three suspects tied to a February shooting at a downtown San Jose business are in custody, police said Thursday. The shooting occurred about 12:45 a.m. on Feb. 15 inside a business in the first block of South Second Street. A man had been arguing with the suspects when one of them pulled out a gun and allegedly shot the victim multiple times, according to police. The victim was transported to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. The suspected shooter, later identified as Sean James Jr., was restrained by employees before being taken into custody by police. A privately-made gun that allegedly belonged to James was found at the scene. The remaining suspects fled before police arrived. James was booked into the Santa Clara County Main Jail on suspicion of attempted murder and various firearm-related crimes. San Jose gang investigations detectives identified the three other suspects as Isaac Ortiz-Guerrero, Marteis Sims and Gabriel Martinez. On May 14, Sims was arrested in San Leandro, and Ortiz-Guerrero and Martinez were arrested in San Jose. While carrying out search warrants, detectives allegedly located gang indicia, as well as evidence of the shooting. In addition, an illegal firearm was allegedly found at Ortiz-Guerrero’s home. All three were booked into Santa Clara County Main Jail on suspicion of attempted murder. Anyone with information about the case or similar cases is asked to contact Detective Moreno #4882 and/or Detective Guevara #4669 of the San Jose Police Department Gang Investigations Unit via email: 4882@sanjoseca.gov and/or 4669@sanjoseca.gov, or (408) 277-3835.

headlines.title: Judge finds teenager responsible for murder in Santana Row fatal stabbing
headlines.description: A Santa Clara County juvenile justice court judge on Friday ruled that a then-13-year-old boy is responsible for second-degree murder following the deadly 2025 Valentine’s Day stabbing of another boy at Santana Row in San Jose. The ruling came after a monthslong juvenile court trial centered on the killing of 15-year-old David Gutierrez, who was attacked on Feb. 14, 2025. Following the ruling, Santa Clara County District Attorney Jeff Rosen said prosecutors will ask the judge to sentence the now-14-year-old defendant to seven years in a secure juvenile facility. Gutierrez’s family said they believe justice will only be served if the sentence amounts to years, not months. “We’re not gonna be scared to speak up,” Gutierrez’s aunt, Dianna, said. “We’re never gonna be scared to speak up for David. So, our family is really, really sad right now (sob) but at the same time we’re gonna celebrate this victory.” Defense attorneys argued during trial that the boy acted in self-defense because he believed Gutierrez had a knife. San Jose police said investigators found no evidence that Gutierrez was armed. It remains unclear how the court could impose the seven-year recommendation under California laws governing juvenile offenders who were 13 or younger at the time of a crime. “The minor was 13.5 at the time,” Rosen said after the ruling. Gutierrez’s mom also spoke outside court Friday, supporting the judge’s decision. “ It’s a tragedy for me and my family… but I don’t know if we will ever be able to recover from it,” Veronica Gutierrez said. “I feel like today the judge did the right thing. She was fair. It was very clear that David got attacked and killed for no reason.” Authorities said the then-13-year-old defendant and four other teenagers confronted Gutierrez because he was wearing red, which they associated with a rival gang. San Jose police said Gutierrez had no gang ties and was wearing red because it was Valentine’s Day. The judge found the boy responsible for the assault and the deadly stabbing that followed moments later. The judge also found the defendant guilty in a separate assault at Valley Fair mall in which prosecutors said he attacked another person and stole a gold necklace and a shoe. Two additional charges remain unresolved. The only adult charged in the case, Emmanuel Sanchez Damian, remains in custody and his case is pending. Two other teens who were 16 at the time of the attack were previously found responsible in Gutierrez’s assault case. One was sentenced to two years in juvenile hall. The now-14-year-old defendant is scheduled to return to court July 1. San Jose May 18 San Jose Mar 20 San Jose Aug 5, 2025 San Jose May 4, 2025 Group holds rally for teen killed in Santana Row stabbing

headlines.title: Caltrans begins final weekend closure of SF's Highway 1 (19th Avenue)
headlines.description: The third and final weekend closure of a busy stretch of San Francisco’s Highway 1 (19th Avenue) began Friday morning. Caltrans shut down Highway 1 (19th Avenue) in both directions between Sloat Boulevard and Holloway Avenue so crews can complete the final phase of a major repaving project. The full closure started at 7 a.m. Friday and is scheduled to last through 10 a.m. Monday. Transportation officials are urging drivers to avoid the area if possible and use alternate routes, including Sloat Boulevard and Junipero Serra Boulevard. Caltrans also asked pedestrians to stay on sidewalks and avoid walking in the street or on medians through the construction zone because of heavy equipment operating in the area. San Francisco May 11 San Francisco May 8 San Francisco Apr 23 Crews have already completed two previous weekend closures as part of the paving work, and newly repaved lanes are visible along parts of the corridor. Caltrans officials said completing the work over three weekend closures will allow crews to finish the project in about 10 days. Officials said the work would have taken about 40 days if lanes had remained partially open during weekday construction.

headlines.title: Waymo suspends robotaxi services on freeways
headlines.description: Waymo suspended its robotaxi service on freeways and paused operations in Atlanta, a spokesperson said Friday. The company says the service suspension is to update software and to improve performance around construction zones and flooded roadways. Waymo has been operating on freeways from San Francisco to the South Bay, as well as in LA, Phoenix and Miami. Waymo says it expects to resume all of the suspended routes soon.

headlines.title: Brentwood high school students hurt while cleaning after experiment
headlines.description: Two Heritage High School students were hurt after an experiment demonstration at Adams Middle School on Friday, according to the Liberty Union High School District. The school district said the incident occurred when Heritage High School students, under the supervision of their teacher, were on the Adams Middle School campus conducting an experiment. After the demonstration, authorities said the students began to clean up when a chemical was spilled, hurting both. Officials said the students were transported by air to the UC Davis Burn Center for evaluation and treatment. The school district adds no other students or staff were injured. The school district also said it will “conduct a throughout review of the incident and its science safety procedures.”

headlines.title: Ebola outbreak concerns grow as fans prepare to travel for the World Cup
headlines.description: The Ebola outbreak is raising global concerns, just as thousands of fans prepare to travel for the World Cup. The Santa Clara County Department of Public Health says it’s been preparing for months ahead of the World Cup, including how to monitor infectious diseases. While health experts say containing the outbreak is critical, it also says the risk of Ebola spreading in the U.S. remains low. Preparations are well underway for the FIFA World Cup across the United States. The tournament will bring hundreds of thousands of visitors from around the world, but as the countdown continues, so does the push to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak in Africa. The CDC reports nearly 600 suspected cases and 148 suspected deaths. That concern reached North America Wednesday, when an Air France flight to Detroit was diverted to Canada after a passenger from the Democratic Republic of Congo was allowed to board in error. The U.S. is now restricting entry for non-U.S. citizens who have been in Congo, South Sudan, or Uganda within the past three weeks. Americans returning from those regions will be routed through Dulles International Airport for enhanced screening. With the World Cup drawing closer, the DRC’s National Team has canceled its pre-tournament camp amid the outbreak. FIFA tells NBC News it’s working with U.S. health agencies and continuing to monitor the situation. The team is still set to play in Houston, with a possible match in Dallas. Meanwhile, The Bay Area is expecting thousands of international visitors for six matches in Santa Clara. The county Department of Public Health says it has a plan in place to monitor people traveling from areas impacted by the outbreak. Health experts also stress that while Ebola is deadly, it does not spread easily like COVID-19. “You have to be super sick and in the middle of an outbreak and around people who are dying and very sick with those bodily fluid exposures to get sick, that’s not gonna be people who are coming here to the World Cup, very sick people are not going to be able to get on a plane, so I’m not worried about Ebola. Am I worried about other things like measles, influenza, anything else that can spread when a lot of people are together? Yes,” said Dr. Monica Gandhi, infectious disease doctor at UCSF. Gandhi also said she believes the U.S. withdrawal from the World Health Organization and USAID cuts may have delayed awareness of the outbreak. The state department denies that claim.

headlines.title: 2 displaced after house fire in South San Jose
headlines.description: Two residents were displaced by a fire Thursday night at a home in South San Jose, fire officials said. The fire was reported about 8:50 p.m. at a two-story home in the 400 block of Tanfield Lane. Crews had the fire knocked down by 9:34 p.m., according to the San Jose Fire Department. The Red Cross was called to assist two people displaced by the fire. Information on what started the fire was not available.
headlines.source: Victoria Meza and Bay City News

headlines.title: For Memorial Day weekend, California gas prices are the highest since 2022
headlines.description: The Memorial Day weekend travel rush has begun, and this year, the holiday weekend comes at a time when consumers are facing rising gas prices. According to AAA, this week, California’s average gas price was $6.14 a gallon, which is the highest the state has seen for this time of year, with the exception of 2022. The average cost of gas in California is up $1.26 from the same time last year, AAA said. AAA notes that this price places California as the market with the highest gas prices in the nation. Doug Johnson, a spokesperson for AAA Northern California, said that with the Strait of Hormuz still closed and with two California refineries recently shuttered, relief is not in sight yet. Plus, he noted that with more people looking to take summer road trips, demand for gas will increase, which could further increase prices. Still, Johnson predicts that many Californians will still opt to drive over the holiday. “They’re still gonna go on their vacation, but they’re going to wanna cut corners where they can,” he said. AAA forecasts that 5,867,966 will travel in California over the holiday, and of those, 4,779,297 people will travel by car. In Foster City, several drivers told NBC Bay Area they are still planning to keep up their Memorial Day plans despite the gas prices. “We’re still gonna keep doing what we have to do, we’re big on spending time with our family, so we’re definitely going to take that trip,” said Jeev Kumar of Foster City, who said it now costs around $160 to fill up the tank of his truck. Laura Callagy of Foster City said she paid $82 to fill up her car on Thursday. But she and her family are still planning to drive up near Yosemite for the weekend. “The air fares are crazy, I mean everything is expensive, so we just are driving, but it’s still very costly- it’s just too expensive, the Bay Area’s too expensive,” Callagy said, adding that they plan to fill up gas closer to Yosemite, where the gas is cheaper. According to AAA, these are the average gas prices for different Northern California cities this week: San Francisco: $6.33 Oakland: $6.22 San Jose: $6.16 Sacramento: $6.10 Fresno: $6.09 Stockton: $6.01 You might be able to save money on gas this holiday weekend by avoiding driving furing peak traffic hours. AAA said that the worst times to travel this holiday weekend will be Thursday and Friday from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m., as well as Monday afternoon. The best times to travel will be morning hours and Sunday, AAA noted.

headlines.title: Firefighters battle grass fire in Contra Costa County
headlines.description: Crews are battling a grass fire in Contra Costa County, according to Cal Fire. Cal Fire said the fire was reported in the area of Nortonville Road and Black Diamond Way near Clayton. The incident, dubbed Norton Fire, started just before 5:30 p.m., according to Cal Fire’s website. Cal Fire reported the flames initially burned up to 10 acres, but later said the fire grew until it reached 20 acres. Just before 7 p.m. Cal Fire stated the fire burned 40 acres, and forward progress has been stopped, but containment has been difficult. Contra Costa County May 21 wildfires May 20 San Jose May 20 The cause of the fire is unknown. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates.
